examples.title: Gram-Schmidt Process
examples.description: A pseudocode implementation of the Gram-Schmidt process
examples.code:
FUNCTION GramSchmidt(vectors)
  FOR EACH vector IN vectors
    orthogonalVector = vector - ∑(v_i, vector) / (v_i, v_i) * v_i
    RETURN orthogonalVector
  END FOR
END FUNCTION
examples.explanation: This pseudocode implements the Gram-Schmidt process to orthogonalize a set of vectors.

examples.title: Gram-Schmidt Process
examples.description: A Python implementation of the Gram-Schmidt process
examples.code:
import numpy as np

def gram_schmidt(vectors):
  orthogonal_vectors = []
  for vector in vectors:
    orthogonal_vector = vector - np.sum(np.dot(vectors, vector) / np.dot(vectors, vectors) * vectors)
    orthogonal_vectors.append(orthogonal_vector)
  return orthogonal_vectors
examples.explanation: This Python code implements the Gram-Schmidt process to orthogonalize a set of vectors.
